repo_notes.content: documentation for personal NixOS infrastructure-as-code configuration

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the Echelon configuration repository, explaining its purpose as a NixOS system configuration for host 'skynet' with a Wayland-based desktop environment

pages.title: System Architecture
pages.purpose: Explain the overall architecture showing how flakes, system configuration, and user configuration work together

pages.title: Nix Flake Infrastructure
pages.purpose: Deep dive into flake.nix and flake.lock, explaining dependency management, input following patterns, and how external packages are integrated

pages.title: NixOS System Configuration
pages.purpose: Document the system-level configuration including bootloader, networking, services, performance tuning, and hardware setup

pages.title: Home Manager Configuration
pages.purpose: Explain the user-level configuration structure, how home.nix aggregates various user modules, and the separation between system and user concerns

pages.title: Desktop Environment
pages.purpose: Overview of the Wayland-based desktop stack from compositor to desktop shell to applications

pages.title: Niri Window Manager
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to Niri configuration including column layout, hotkeys, input settings, and window rules

pages.title: Noctalia Desktop Shell
pages.purpose: Document the Noctalia shell configuration including bar widgets, notifications, wallpaper management, and OSD settings

pages.title: Kitty Terminal Emulator
pages.purpose: Detail Kitty terminal configuration including font, theme, keybindings, tab bar, and shell integration

pages.title: Alternative Compositors
pages.purpose: Document alternative window manager/compositor configurations including Mango and Cosmic

pages.title: Text Editors
pages.purpose: Overview of the three primary text editor configurations and their respective features

pages.title: Helix Editor
pages.purpose: Document Helix configuration including LSP setup, language servers, formatters, and keybindings

pages.title: Neovim
pages.purpose: Explain Neovim configuration with Mini.nvim plugin manager, init.lua structure, and plugin organization

pages.title: Doom Emacs
pages.purpose: Detail Doom Emacs configuration including enabled modules, custom settings, and package management

pages.title: Shell Environments
pages.purpose: Document shell configurations and their shared aliases, focusing on Fish as the primary shell

pages.title: Fish Shell
pages.purpose: Detail Fish shell configuration including aliases for git/nix/make, plugins, and interactive features

pages.title: Alternative Shells
pages.purpose: Document configurations for Nushell, Bash, and Ion shells

pages.title: Command-Line Tools
pages.purpose: Overview of the extensive CLI toolkit including search, file management, and development utilities

pages.title: Search and Navigation
pages.purpose: Document ripgrep, fd, fzf, bat, and yazi configurations for file searching and navigation

pages.title: Version Control Tools
pages.purpose: Explain Git configuration with delta integration and Jujutsu VCS setup

pages.title: System Monitoring
pages.purpose: Document bottom (btm) system monitor configuration

pages.title: Development Environments
pages.purpose: Overview of development shells for different programming languages and formatters

pages.title: Rust Development
pages.purpose: Document Rust development shells including eframe (WASM+GUI) and general Rust with GTK4

pages.title: Other Language Environments
pages.purpose: Document development shells for Nim and ATmega32 embedded development

pages.title: Code Formatters
pages.purpose: Explain system-wide formatter installation (stylua, shfmt, alejandra) and their integration with editors

pages.title: Configuration Patterns
pages.purpose: Explain common configuration patterns used throughout the system

pages.title: Module Aggregation
pages.purpose: Document the pattern of using aggregator files (source.nix, home.nix, editors.nix) to compose modular configurations

pages.title: Symlink Management
pages.purpose: Explain the out-of-store symlink pattern for managing dotfiles (niri, doom, nvim, mpv, ewwii)

pages.title: Flake Input Following
pages.purpose: Detail the dependency management strategy where all inputs follow the root nixpkgs to prevent version conflicts

pages.title: Specialized Services
pages.purpose: Document specialized system services including AI/ML and performance optimization
